entries.title: Gwen
entries.summary: Gwen is the main protagonist of Tales of the Tavern. She is a 23-year-old human woman from Osul who used to work with her uncle before being recruited by Tohm Kyatt to work at his tavern in Neverue / Whisker Woods.
entries.publicDescription: Gwen is a hardworking young fighter from Osul who finds herself swept into a strange culinary adventure after joining Tohm Kyatt's tavern. Armed with her sword, basket, and appetite, she gathers ingredients, protects villages, and cooks magical meals to face the dangers spreading through Whisker Woods.
entries.internalLore: Gwen is tomboyish, hardworking, smart, a talented fighter, and protective of villages. She goes out to farm, gather, complete tasks, fight critters, and protect people with her sword. She has an ale addiction she is working on. Her favorite food is anything potatoes. She is recruited by Tohm Kyatt to help recover stolen/torn recipes and stop the spread of magical corruption.

fields.Personality: Direct, practical, slightly sarcastic, brave, protective, easily annoyed by nonsense, but kind underneath.
fields.Gameplay Role: Player character. She gathers ingredients, fights enemies, cooks meals, equips meals for combat buffs and abilities, completes quests, explores Whisker Woods, and recovers recipe pages.
fields.Visual Notes: In-game Gwen has a big nose, but for cinematic/anime-style marketing her nose should be drawn smaller because the original look does not translate as well visually. She should feel strong, grounded, expressive, practical, and charming.
fields.Pose Suggestions: Hands on hips, leaning on sword, carrying basket, rummaging through basket, protective combat stance, tired but determined, annoyed at Tohm, smiling after a good meal.

entries.title: Tohm Kyatt
entries.summary: Tohm Kyatt is a world-renowned Whisken chef and food critic who discovers the Cat Cauldron, awakens its power, and later hires Gwen to recover torn magical recipe pages.
entries.publicDescription: Tohm Kyatt is a world-renowned Whisken chef and food critic whose mysterious recipes may be the key to saving the land. Brilliant, dramatic, and more than a little secretive, Tohm recruits Gwen to help recover stolen recipes before they cause even more chaos.
entries.internalLore: Tohm Kyatt is a Whisken cat chef and food critic. He is the only cat to ever possess sweet taste buds, making him obsessed with tasting every possible food. As a child, his mother told him stories of the Tablemaker, the canonical divine figure also called the Master Chef, and those stories shaped his obsession with cooking, sacred meals, and the knowledge of what is untasted. Tohm originally lived on Tabby Island with the Whisken people. Later in life, he discovered that the ancient Whisken had created the Cat Cauldron beneath Tabby Island and erased it from their own history after it caused the island's first decay and first exodus. Tohm returned to Tabby Island, found the Cat Cauldron, and cooked a meal in it. Activating it released a large pulse into the earth, causing Tabby Island to begin decaying again and allowing the Mas'eel Cult to sense the Cat Cauldron's power. Tohm then took the Cat Cauldron and fled in the Living Tavern without anyone knowing. When he later heard that Tabby Island had become corrupted and that the Whisken were being persecuted by Mas'eel infiltrators, he got his friend Lel Kai, who was becoming general of the faery army, to send boats and save as many people as possible. Tohm is not purely evil. He is flawed, obsessive, secretive, prideful, and morally compromised, but he has a redemption arc. He wants to fix the harm caused by his obsession. Important canon detail: Tohm never drinks from the cauldron. Updated Lillia Incident: Tohm creates an unstable magical dish using the dragon knife and magical culinary knowledge. Lillia consumes it and gains dangerous magical powers. The living chicken tavern suddenly stands up, causing Lillia, who wears large pajamas resembling a witch costume, to fall over. This gives Tohm the opportunity to snatch the recipe book back, but Lillia rips out several pages. As guards approach, Tohm is forced to flee.

fields.Motivation: To taste the impossible, master magical cooking, and eventually fix the damage his obsession caused.
fields.Arc: From obsessive culinary genius hiding from his consequences to flawed mentor seeking redemption.
fields.Gameplay Role: Quest giver, mentor, chef, lore source, recipe system anchor, morally complex guide.
fields.Faith Link: Raised on stories of the Tablemaker, also called the Master Chef.
fields.Canon Note: Discarded older mythic names are not canon entities.

secrets: Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
Secret: Tohm Never Drinks From The Cauldron
timelineEvents: Lillia Consumes the Unstable Magical Dish

entries.title: Princess Lillia
entries.summary: Princess Lillia is a stubborn young princess of the human kingdom who grew up wanting to become a faery. Her obsession with magic leads to the spread of the Dark Culinary Arts.
entries.publicDescription: Princess Lillia is a magical threat tied to the rise of corrupted cooking and dangerous recipe-powered enemies. Her obsession with magic has left a trail of strange meals, twisted powers, and chaos across the land.
entries.internalLore: Lillia is the daughter of the king and queen. Because the royal family was close to the faery kingdom, Lillia grew up fascinated by faeries and eventually became obsessed with becoming one. The king and queen spent about a year consulting mages and allies to find a way to give her magical powers. The faeries refused to help because they feared humans gaining magic would lead to humans dominating all kingdoms. The king eventually turned to the dwarven kingdom, knowing they possessed a magical dragon knife. The dwarves refused to give it up, and the king declared war. After winning, he took the dragon knife. Tohm Kyatt eventually gains access to the dragon knife through the royal food contest / royal access plotline. He creates an unstable magical dish. Lillia consumes it and gains dangerous magical powers. She attempts to steal Tohm's recipe book, but only tears out several pages before Tohm escapes. The torn recipe pages become central to the game. Lillia later sets up camp in the faery realm because she can consume the magic in the environment to mass-produce Dark Culinary Arts instead of infusing each meal one at a time. The Mas'eel Cult now works with her while searching for the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's magical recipes.

fields.Dark Culinary Arts: Lillia uses magic to corrupt food. Food infused with the Dark Culinary Arts can make consumers evil or corrupted. She distributes magically enhanced dishes to people who later become bosses Gwen must defeat.
fields.Personality: Stubborn, entitled, obsessive, magical, theatrical, dangerous, childish in some ways but terrifying because of her power.
fields.Gameplay Role: Major antagonist. Source of corrupted magical meals and recipe-powered bosses.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages

entries.title: Lel Kai
entries.summary: Lel Kai is a character connected to Tales of the Tavern's faery/magical side and appears in plans for Whisker Woods content and marketing scenes.
entries.internalLore: Lel Kai has a human form and is connected to the fairy/faery kingdom side of the world. Later, as Lel Kai is becoming general of the faery army, Tohm conveniently gets them to send boats to rescue the Whisken from Tabby Island during the second exodus. The corruption is severe enough that many boats are dispersed, and the known survivors are the Whisken who reach Whisker Woods. Lel Kai is planned to appear in the Whisker Woods vertical slice and later story progression. In New Year marketing art, Lel Kai appears at the fairy kingdom popping champagne with fairy dust in the sky like fireworks.

notes.production: Needs idle and walk animations for playtest scope. May appear after the Thairrott mini-boss in a planned vertical slice meet-up with Cedar and Lyra.

entries.title: Oswin
entries.summary: Oswin is an alchemist elder, village remedy-maker, and inventor of Healthy Ale who is suspicious of Tohm Kyatt.
entries.internalLore: Oswin functions as a skeptical elder/alchemist figure who may understand more about magic, food, corruption, or Tohm's past than most villagers. He is suspicious of Tohm and can serve as a counterweight to Tohm's secrecy. Notion lore also gives him the village-famous Healthy Ale, a remedy-like drink that Whisken villagers rely on even though the name is funnier than it is medically accurate.

fields.Gameplay Role: Potential NPC, lore explainer, alchemy/crafting system connection, quest giver.

secrets: Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron

entries.title: Kap
entries.summary: Kap is a generous Whisken fisherman tied to the corrupted pond opening quest and dreams of setting sail someday.
entries.internalLore: Kap is a villager and fisherman who gets attacked or endangered near the corrupted pond. Gwen hears him screaming for help, fights bugs, and eventually faces a prawnhusk mini-boss. Notion lore frames Kap as someone who loves the ocean, dreams of setting sail, and shares his catches generously with the village.

fields.Gameplay Role: Opening quest NPC, fishing system connection, emotional reason to care about the village.
fields.Personal Dream: Sail beyond Whisker Woods and see the wider waters.

entries.title: King
entries.summary: The human king is Lillia's father. His obsession with granting her magic leads him to war with the dwarves and obtain the dragon knife.
entries.internalLore: The king loves Lillia but becomes increasingly obsessed with fulfilling her wish to gain magic. After the faeries refuse to help, he turns to the dwarves, who possess the magical dragon knife. When they refuse to give it up, he declares war, wins, and takes the knife. This act sets the stage for Tohm's involvement and Lillia's eventual corruption.

secrets: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War

entries.title: Queen
entries.summary: The queen is Lillia's mother and part of the royal family's attempt to grant Lillia magical powers.
entries.internalLore: The queen indulges Lillia's obsession alongside the king and supports the search for magical solutions. She is connected to the royal family's year-long effort to give Lillia magic.

entries.title: The Tablemaker and Triadic Faith
entries.summary: The Tablemaker is the canonical divine figure of the setting, also called the Master Chef, and most cultures follow the Triadic faith in their own traditions.
entries.internalLore: The Tablemaker is the canonical one God of the world. The Master Chef is another name for him, not a separate being. Most races now worship the Tablemaker and follow the Triadic faith, though each race practices the faith through its own local traditions, foods, feasts, rites, and cultural habits. The core triad is Passion, Taste, and Love. The Whisken people are strongly tied to this faith, and Tohm grew up listening to stories of the Tablemaker before his obsession with magical cooking and the Cat Cauldron took hold.

fields.Cultural Practice: Most races believe in the same God and same faith, but practice it differently through their own traditions.
fields.Canon Note: Discarded older mythic names are not canon figures.

entries.title: Whisker Woods
entries.summary: Whisker Woods is a major early-game region where the Whisken survivors live after fleeing Tabby Island.
entries.internalLore: Whisker Woods is a cozy but increasingly corrupted forest region. It includes cliffs, flowers, grass, trees, village areas, ponds, bug nests, groves, farms, and magical points of interest. It is the main setting for the Act 1 / vertical slice playtest.

fields.Visual Identity: Warm earthy colors, bold outlines, whimsical forest shapes, tavern/cozy fantasy atmosphere, charming but with pockets of corruption.
fields.Gameplay Purpose: Opening exploration area, tutorial region, gathering zone, enemy encounters, cooking resources, village hub, corrupted pond event, bug nest blockade, Act 1 boss buildup.

entries.title: Whisken Village
entries.summary: Village area in Whisker Woods where the Whisken survivors live.
entries.internalLore: A village culture built around taverns, cooking, hunting, farming, fishing, gathering, and community survival. The village represents the cozy heart of the game and shows how food is tied to identity and daily life.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: NPC hub, quest hub, cooking/tavern connection, social location.

entries.title: Tabby Island
entries.summary: The original home of the Whisken people, twice struck by decay tied to the Cat Cauldron and later infiltrated by the Mas'eel Cult.
entries.internalLore: Tabby Island was the original home of the Whisken people, including Tohm Kyatt. In ancient times, Whisken seekers created the Cat Cauldron while trying to improve food and reach the knowledge of what is untasted. Its power caused the island to begin decaying, leading to the first exodus. The ancient Whisken locked the Cat Cauldron away at the bottom of the island, erased it from history books, and stopped speaking about it until it was forgotten by the time their people returned. Later, Tohm discovered the buried knowledge, returned to Tabby Island, cooked a meal in the Cat Cauldron, and released a pulse into the earth that started the island decaying again. Tohm fled with the Cat Cauldron in the Living Tavern without anyone knowing. The pulse also let the Mas'eel Cult sense the artifact, and cultists came to Tabby Island pretending to be traders before slowly searing the island, gaining power in the village, and persecuting the Whisken for their Triadic faith.

fields.Gameplay / Story Purpose: Major backstory location, source of Tohm's guilt, Mas'eel infiltration site, potential future reveal location or memory sequence.
fields.First Exodus: Ancient Whisken fled after the Cat Cauldron caused Tabby Island to decay.
fields.Second Exodus: Later Whisken fled again after Tohm reawakened the Cat Cauldron and the Mas'eel Cult corrupted the island.

secrets: Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
Secret: Cat Cauldron Beneath Tabby Island
Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island

entries.title: Kap's Pond
entries.summary: A fishing pond in Whisker Woods where Kap is attacked during an early quest.
entries.internalLore: Normal: peaceful fishing spot. Corrupted: dark water, bugs, Kap in danger, prawnhusk emerges. Purified: fish return, villagers may revisit, slime formation may occur.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Opening quest event, combat tutorial, fishing system connection, corrupted zone demonstration.

entries.title: Faery Realm
entries.summary: A magical realm connected to faeries and Lillia's later use of the Dark Culinary Arts.
entries.internalLore: Lillia sets up camp in the faery realm because she can consume the magic in the faery environment to mass-produce the Dark Culinary Arts. If she stayed in the castle, she would need to infuse each meal one at a time.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Uses Dark Culinary Arts

entries.title: Human Kingdom / Royal Castle
entries.summary: The kingdom ruled by Lillia's parents. The royal family's desire to grant Lillia magic leads to war and the dragon knife incident.

secrets: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War

entries.title: Dwarven Mountains
entries.summary: The region where the dwarves possessed the magical dragon knife before the human king took it through war.

secrets: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War

entries.title: Saltlick Stones
entries.summary: A Whisker Woods point of interest connected to minerals, salt, wildlife, or food gathering.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Gathering area, environmental storytelling, possible mineral/salt ingredient source.

entries.title: Mushroom Ring Glade
entries.summary: A magical forest glade with mushrooms, possible faery activity, ingredients, slimes, or hidden secrets.

entries.title: Honey Hollow
entries.summary: A sweet resource area with honey, bees, sticky hazards, and possible sweet-flavored slimes.

entries.title: Firefly Meadow
entries.summary: A glowing meadow that can show the beauty and magic of Whisker Woods, especially at night.

entries.title: Dark Culinary Arts
entries.summary: A corrupted magical cooking practice used by Lillia to infuse food with dark magic.
entries.internalLore: The Dark Culinary Arts allow food to be infused with corruptive magic. Consumers of these meals can become evil, corrupted, or gain dangerous powers. Lillia uses the magic of the faery realm to mass-produce these corrupted meals and distribute them to individuals who later become bosses.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Explains corrupted bosses, recipe-powered enemies, dark meals, and magical food as both power and danger.

secrets: Secret: Dark Culinary Arts Can Corrupt Consumers
timelineEvents: Lillia Begins Using Dark Culinary Arts

entries.title: Magical Meals
entries.summary: Meals that grant Gwen temporary combat powers, stat boosts, ultimate abilities, or special effects.
entries.internalLore: Players gather ingredients, prepare them, cook meals, and equip meals in a meal slot wheel. Gwen can consume meals mid-battle for temporary powers.

entries.title: Cray Broth
entries.summary: A savory aquatic broth made from Crayhusk meat, specialty herbs, and boga.

fields.Preparation: Drag valid ingredients into the cauldron, boil, and stir slowly as it cooks.
fields.Gameplay Effect: Could provide defensive, aquatic, or savory/umami buffs.

entries.title: Veggie Broth
entries.summary: A comforting vegetable broth made from specialty herbs, boga, and turnip.

fields.Preparation: Boil ingredients in cauldron and stir slowly.
fields.Gameplay Effect: Could provide healing, stamina, or comfort buffs.

entries.title: Fire Meal
entries.summary: A spicy magical meal that could give Gwen fire-based combat abilities.

fields.Gameplay Effect: Could give Gwen fire attacks, burn damage, warmth resistance, or explosive cooking energy.

entries.title: Ales / Tonics
entries.summary: Ales and tonics provide buffs, heals, or gameplay advantages.
entries.internalLore: Gwen has an ale addiction she is working on, so ale has both gameplay and character-story relevance.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Healing, buffs, status effects, tavern economy, character depth.

entries.title: Slime Substitutions
entries.summary: Slimes can sometimes substitute for ingredients based on flavor.
entries.internalLore: Slimes are born from excess nutrients released through roots in ripe and healthy forests. These nutrients gather in ponds and slowly form into slimes. Their flavors come from whatever plants are abundant near the pond. Strawberry slime can substitute for strawberry, blueberry slime for blueberry, etc. Slimes can also be brewed into ales. Elemental slimes are tied to flavor profiles. Dark elemental slimes come from excess corruption rather than healthy forest nutrients.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Ingredient substitution, ale brewing, flavor/element system, resource gathering.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.summary: Gwen's favorite food category.

entries.title: Turnip
entries.summary: Root vegetable used in veggie broth.

entries.summary: Ingredient used in broths. Needs more lore.

entries.summary: Herbs used in broths, seasoning, and magical meals.

entries.summary: Meat from Crayhusk enemy.

entries.summary: Meat from Prawnhusk enemy.

entries.summary: Sweet slime ingredient substitute.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.title: Blueberry Slime Gel
entries.summary: Sweet/tart slime ingredient substitute.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.title: Dark Slime Essence
entries.summary: Corrupted slime essence from areas of excess corruption.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.title: Ale Ingredients
entries.summary: Ingredients used for brewing buffs/heals.

entries.title: Opening Grocery Quest
entries.summary: Gwen finishes a meal and must gather ingredients for Tohm's menu before a deadline.

fields.Quest Flow: 1. Gwen finishes a meal and reacts that it was awesome. 2. She references Tohm's harsh critique and deadline to bring new recipe/menu by 8pm. 3. Gwen receives or checks a grocery list. 4. The player gathers ingredients like boar meat, Purfox, Sunchee, or other starter ingredients. 5. The route introduces gathering, movement, and early exploration. 6. A bug nest blockade interrupts the route and introduces combat.
fields.Gameplay Tutorials: Gathering, quest tracker, basic movement, possibly inventory.

entries.title: Kap's Pond Rescue
entries.summary: Gwen hears Kap screaming for help near a corrupted pond and fights bugs before a prawnhusk mini-boss appears.

fields.Quest Flow: 1. Player approaches pond. 2. Kap screams for help. 3. Gwen reaches corrupted pond. 4. Small bug enemies attack. 5. Prawnhusk mini-boss appears. 6. Gwen defeats it. 7. Kap thanks Gwen and heads to the tavern. 8. Pond may eventually become purified.
fields.Gameplay Tutorials: Combat, enemy waves, mini-boss, healing ale, corruption introduction.
fields.Lore Revealed: Corruption is spreading through Whisker Woods. Ponds and wildlife can be affected.

entries.title: Craft a Pickaxe Tutorial
entries.summary: When the player tries to break rocks with a sword, the game teaches pickaxe crafting.

fields.Flow: 1. Player attacks rock with sword. 2. Prompt appears explaining that a pickaxe is needed. 3. Crafting menu opens or is introduced. 4. Player collects sticks and stones. 5. Player crafts a basic pickaxe. 6. Player breaks rock.

entries.title: Craft a Slingshot Tutorial
entries.summary: When the player finds fruit trees, the game teaches slingshot crafting.

fields.Flow: Player sees fruit out of reach, crafts slingshot, shoots fruit down.

entries.title: Craft a Shovel Tutorial
entries.summary: When the player finds dirt mounds/root vegetables, the game teaches shovel crafting.

entries.title: Cave Entrance Tutorial
entries.summary: When the player steps near the cave entrance, the game introduces cave exploration naturally without needing an exclamation icon.

entries.title: Recover Recipe Pages
entries.summary: Gwen must defeat individuals empowered by magical recipe pages to recover Tohm's torn recipes.
entries.internalLore: Bosses have powers connected to the recipes they consumed or were corrupted by. After defeating bosses, Gwen retrieves recipe pages and unlocks new powers/meals.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages

entries.title: Slimes
entries.summary: Slimes are creatures born from excess nutrients. They come in different flavors and behaviors.
entries.internalLore: When a forest is ripe and healthy, it disposes of extra nutrients through plant roots. These nutrients gather in ponds and slowly form into slimes. The flavor of a slime comes from whatever plants are abundant around that pond. Some slimes attack on sight. Some are neutral. Some only attack if attacked. Some run away. Slimes can be used as ingredient substitutes, sweeteners, and ale brewing materials. Elements are tied to flavor profiles. Dark elemental slimes are born from excess corruption in an area. Examples: red slime = strawberry flavor, blue slime = blueberry flavor, dark slime = corruption flavor/profile.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.title: Prawnhusk
entries.summary: An aquatic/crustacean-like enemy tied to corrupted pond encounters.
entries.internalLore: Appears as a mini-boss during Kap's Pond Rescue and drops Prawnhusk meat.

entries.title: Crayhusk
entries.summary: A crawfish/crayfish-like enemy that can drop meat used in broths.
entries.internalLore: Drops Crayhusk meat and relates to aquatic enemy families.

entries.summary: Basic bug enemy planned for Whisker Woods playtest.

entries.title: Beetle Enemy 2
entries.summary: Second beetle variation planned for Whisker Woods playtest.

entries.title: Fly Bug
entries.summary: Flying bug enemy planned for Whisker Woods playtest.

entries.title: Sword Skelly
entries.summary: Skeleton sword enemy planned for playtest scope.

entries.title: Boar
entries.summary: Boar enemy/resource creature tied to meat gathering and possible magical boar boss idea.

entries.title: Chicken
entries.summary: Chicken creature/enemy/resource creature.

entries.title: Ice Queen
entries.summary: Act 1 final boss, queen of cursed bugs in Whisker Woods.

fields.Gameplay Purpose: Major boss encounter for Act 1. Could represent the peak of bug corruption.

entries.title: Magical Boar Boss
entries.summary: A magical boar boss in a grove.

entries.title: Were-Dog NPC Boss
entries.summary: An NPC boss triggered by night or meal conditions.

fields.Gameplay Concept: Could connect day/night cycle, food, curse mechanics, and player choice.

entries.title: Aquatic Monster Boss
entries.summary: A boss spawned by rare bait.

fields.Gameplay Concept: Ties fishing, rare ingredients, water areas, and boss encounters together.

entries.title: Whisken People
entries.summary: Cat-like people connected to Tabby Island, Whisker Woods, tavern culture, food, hunting, farming, fishing, community, and the Triadic faith.
entries.internalLore: The Whisken people originally lived on Tabby Island. In ancient times, Whisken seekers created the Cat Cauldron while looking for ways to improve food and achieve knowledge of what is untasted. The Cat Cauldron caused Tabby Island to decay, triggering the first exodus. The ancient Whisken locked it away beneath the island, removed it from their history books, stopped talking about it, and eventually forgot it. By the time later Whisken returned to Tabby Island, the first exodus had fallen out of memory. After Tohm Kyatt reactivated the Cat Cauldron and fled with it, the island started decaying again. Before the Whisken understood what was happening, the Mas'eel arrived pretending to be traders, introduced new foods, gained power in the village over years, and began persecuting the Whisken for believing in the Triadic faith taught by the Tablemaker. The Whisken fled again, though to them it felt like the first time. Tohm heard what was happening and got Lel Kai, who was becoming general of the faery army, to send boats. The corruption scattered many boats, and the known survivors became the current Whisken who live in Whisker Woods and Whisken Village.

fields.Faith: The Whisken follow the Tablemaker and the Triadic faith.
fields.Faith Practice: Like most races, they believe in the same God and faith but practice it through their own traditions.
fields.Known Refugees: The current Whisken in Whisker Woods / Whisken Village are the known survivors.

secrets: Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
Secret: Cat Cauldron Beneath Tabby Island
Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island
timelineEvents: Cat Cauldron Is Buried and Forgotten

entries.title: Faery Kingdom
entries.summary: The faeries are magical allies of the human royal family but refuse to help give humans magic.
entries.internalLore: The faeries fear that if humans gain magic, they will become too powerful and potentially dominate or destroy everything. Their refusal pushes the king toward desperate measures, including the war with the dwarves.

secrets: Secret: Faeries Refused Human Magic

entries.title: Human Kingdom
entries.summary: The kingdom ruled by Lillia's parents. Humans are already dominant, and gaining magic would threaten the world balance.

secrets: Secret: Faeries Refused Human Magic
Secret: King's Obsession Caused War

entries.title: Dwarven Kingdom
entries.summary: The dwarves possessed the dragon knife before the human king took it through war.

secrets: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War

entries.title: Mas'eel Cult
entries.summary: A cult that perverts the Triadic faith into FEAST and hunts the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's magical recipes.
entries.internalLore: The core world faith is the Triadic faith of Passion, Taste, and Love. The Mas'eel Cult corrupts this into FEAST. Their symbol is a single distorted dot or eye, opposing the three dots of the triad. When Tohm activated the Cat Cauldron on Tabby Island, the pulse let the Mas'eel sense its power and know it was on the island. Cultists sailed there pretending to be traders, introduced new foods, and spent years secretly searing the island, gaining power inside the Whisken village, and persecuting Whisken people for believing in the Triadic faith taught by the Tablemaker. After the second exodus, the Mas'eel left Tabby Island and began searching for the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's magical recipes while working with Princess Lillia.

fields.Doctrine: FEAST, a distorted compression of Passion, Taste, and Love into consumption and control.
fields.Symbol: One distorted dot or eye, opposing the three-dot Triadic symbol.
fields.Tactics: False trade, new foods, gentle language, slow influence, searing corruption, and faith persecution.
fields.Possible Influence: Leirbag is Notion-sourced and needs review before becoming hard canon.

secrets: Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island
Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron

entries.title: Mur'amar
entries.summary: Mur'amar is a Mas'eel-linked figure whose name spelling is canonical as Mur'amar.
entries.internalLore: Mur'amar is tied to the Mas'eel Cult and should use this spelling going forward. He can present himself as peaceful or benevolent while still serving the cult's search for the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's magical recipes. Notion lore has him moving among Whisken villagers as if he belongs and speaking to Gwen about the Mas'eel faith as something gentle. He may wear or carry Mas'eel signs openly because the current Whisken do not understand what the symbol means.

fields.Public Mask: Gentle stranger / Swan of Peace figure.
fields.Questions He Asks: Cat Cauldron, Tohm's recipes, and what Gwen knows about magical cooking.

secrets: Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island

entries.title: Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: An ancient Whisken cauldron created to improve food and discover what is untasted, later awakened by Tohm and hunted by the Mas'eel Cult.
entries.internalLore: The ancient Whisken created the Cat Cauldron while looking for ways to improve food and achieve knowledge of what is untasted. Its power caused Tabby Island to begin decaying, creating the first exodus. The Whisken locked it away at the bottom of the island, removed it from their history books, and stopped talking about it until it was forgotten. Tohm later discovered the hidden knowledge, returned to Tabby Island, cooked a meal in the Cat Cauldron, and activated it. The cauldron released a large pulse into the earth, causing Tabby Island to decay again and letting the Mas'eel Cult sense its power. Tohm took the Cat Cauldron and fled in the Living Tavern without anyone knowing. Important: Tohm never drinks from the cauldron.

secrets: Secret: Cat Cauldron Beneath Tabby Island
Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
Secret: Tohm Never Drinks From The Cauldron
timelineEvents: Ancient Whisken Create the Cat Cauldron
Cat Cauldron Is Buried and Forgotten

entries.title: Dragon Knife
entries.summary: A magical knife originally possessed by the dwarves, later taken by the human king and used in the chain of events that gave Lillia powers.
entries.internalLore: The king took the dragon knife after declaring war on the dwarves. Tohm gained access to it through the royal setup / cooking contest and used it in preparing magical ingredients. It became a key tool in creating the unstable magical dish Lillia consumed.

secrets: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War
timelineEvents: Tohm Gains Access to Dragon Knife

entries.title: Tohm's Recipe Book
entries.summary: Tohm's complex magical recipe book. Lillia tears out several pages from it.
entries.internalLore: The recipes are too complex for even Tohm to fully memorize. Lillia attempts to steal the book but only tears out several pages when Tohm snatches it back. These recipe pages become the basis for boss powers and Gwen's progression.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages

entries.title: Recipe Pages
entries.summary: Torn pages from Tohm's recipe book that grant or explain magical powers.
entries.internalLore: Gwen retrieves recipe pages by defeating bosses. Each page may unlock a new magical meal, combat power, or progression ability.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages

entries.title: Living Chicken Tavern
entries.summary: A living tavern with chicken legs that becomes Tohm's home/workshop.
entries.internalLore: Tohm discovers or partners with a living tavern with chicken legs in a distant barren land or during his post-disaster journey. During the Lillia incident, the tavern suddenly stands up, causing Lillia to fall and allowing Tohm to snatch the recipe book back.

secrets: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages

entries.title: Gwen's Basket
entries.summary: Gwen's inventory is represented by a basket. When the inventory opens, the camera zooms in and Gwen rummages through it.
entries.internalLore: Redesign inventory as a top-down view into the basket with inventory slots inside it. The crafting menu could pop out to the left of Gwen while the inventory appears on the right.

entries.title: Cooking System
entries.summary: The main gameplay system where players gather ingredients, prepare them, cook meals, and use meals for buffs, powers, and progression.
entries.internalLore: Players can slice, chop, crush, boil, fry, brew, and combine ingredients. Prepared ingredients become new inventory items, such as sliced potato or pan-fried meat. Some ingredients only work with certain prep stations. For example, bread cannot be boiled, meat cannot be crushed, etc. Cauldron task: the player drags valid ingredients into the cauldron and stirs slowly as it boils. Mixing ingredients creates different outcomes, such as Cray Broth or Veggie Broth. Frying task: the player drags valid mostly chopped/sliced ingredients into the pan, waits until the food browns on one side, then holds and drags upward quickly to flip. The pan itself should move during the flip and the object should bounce up and back into the pan. Then the player drags right to remove it and add it to inventory.

entries.title: Meal Slot Wheel
entries.summary: After cooking a meal, Gwen can access a meal slot wheel and consume meals mid-battle for temporary powers, stat boosts, and ultimate abilities.

entries.title: Crafting System
entries.summary: Gwen can craft tools and items from gathered resources.
entries.internalLore: Tools: axe for wood/trees, pickaxe for stone/crystals, sickle for grass/bushes with swords also able to gather some but sickles giving better yield, shovel for dirt mounds/root vegetables, slingshot for fruit trees or distant gathering, torch with durability for dark areas, glowglob lantern as a waist-light made from processed resources. Tools should have linear upgrades such as wood, stone, iron, magical, etc.

entries.title: Inventory System
entries.summary: Inventory appears beside Gwen as the camera zooms in and Gwen rummages through her basket.
entries.internalLore: Redesign as a top-down basket UI with inventory slots inside the basket. Newly prepared ingredients should go into the next available empty slot so players can easily identify what they just made.

entries.title: Quest System
entries.summary: The game uses a quest tracker with objectives, dialogue, inventory checks, and quest states.
entries.internalLore: The project uses Story Framework 5 from FAB for quests, dialogue, interaction, and HasItem inventory checks.

entries.title: Combat System
entries.summary: Gwen fights using a sword and bow mapped directly to buttons without needing to switch in the inventory.

entries.title: Day / Night and Seasons
entries.summary: The game has a year-round calendar with day/night cycle, seasons, weather, and dynamic visual changes.
entries.internalLore: Seasonal swaps can change colors, materials, particles, foliage, and available resources.

entries.title: Slime Flavor / Element System
entries.summary: Slimes connect flavor profiles, ingredients, and elements. Elements are tied to flavor profiles rather than generic elemental categories.

secrets: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption

entries.title: Charm System
entries.summary: Players can find, buy, or win charms and equip up to 3 at a time.
entries.internalLore: Examples: Leecher's Stone attacks heal you for 5% of the damage dealt. Striker's Ring attacks deal 20% more damage. Other ideas: increased speed, two dashes, heal when dashing, chance enemies heal you when killed, faster ranged attack speed, auto-pick vegetable charm.

entries.title: The Tablemaker
entries.summary: The Tablemaker is the canonical divine figure of the setting, also called the Master Chef, whose meal ended the 300 Year War.
entries.internalLore: The Tablemaker arrived during the final years of the 300 Year War between Ovenhold and the Faery Realm. He came not as a soldier, king, or mage, but as one who could prepare a table for enemies. His final meal perfectly contained Passion, Taste, and Love. It ended the war, cost him his mortal life, and released Food Essence into the world before his spirit returned to The Everfeast. The Master Chef is another name for him, not a separate being.

fields.World Impact: Ended the 300 Year War and made magical cooking sacred.

timelineEvents: The Meal That Ended the War

entries.title: The Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: The Cat Cauldron is a sentient cooking station and character tied to broth, meal finalization, Whisken history, and Tohm's hidden disaster.
entries.internalLore: The Cat Cauldron should be tracked as both an artifact and a character. In gameplay terms, it is the sentient cauldron used for broth bases, simmered meals, and certain final meal steps. In lore, ancient Whisken created it while seeking better food and the knowledge of what is untasted. Its awakening caused pulses of decay on Tabby Island and drew the Mas'eel Cult's attention after Tohm cooked in it.

fields.Gameplay Role: Cooking station for broths, meal finalization, and magical recipe moments.
fields.Character Role: Sentient artifact companion / dangerous lore witness.
fields.Canon Link: Same entity as the Cat Cauldron artifact entry.

secrets: Secret: Cat Cauldron Beneath Tabby Island
Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
timelineEvents: Ancient Whisken Create the Cat Cauldron

entries.title: Mona the Orchardist
entries.summary: Mona is a reserved young Whisken orchardist who loves her family but dreams of seeing the world beyond Whisken Village.
entries.internalLore: Mona is connected to Whisken Village's orchards and food-gathering culture. She is quiet and thoughtful, often keeping her thoughts to herself. She loves her family and village, but dreams of exploring the world beyond Whisker Woods. Her orchard work makes her a natural link between pantry ingredients, village routines, and Gwen's early gathering quests.

fields.Story Use: Can introduce fruit, harvest timing, and the emotional cost of staying versus leaving.

entries.title: Momon
entries.summary: Momon is a hardworking Whisken farmer who passes down agricultural knowledge and anchors village food production.
entries.internalLore: Momon works the Whisken Village farm and treats food production as both survival and tradition. He understands Mona's longing for the wider world, but his own life is rooted in the land, the harvest, and keeping the village fed after the exodus.

fields.Story Use: Can explain fields, harvest ingredients, and practical Whisken food customs.

entries.title: Lady Kiko
entries.summary: Lady Kiko is a guiding Whisken presence associated with unity, protection, and the heart of Whisken Village.
entries.internalLore: Lady Kiko is remembered as a mysterious but trusted presence in Whisken Village. She can function as a protector, mediator, or cultural guide who helps express Whisken unity, the Festival of Full Plates, and the quiet practice of the Triadic faith through food and community.

fields.Faith Link: Triadic faith practiced through food, hospitality, and protection.
fields.Story Use: Can help Gwen understand Whisken customs and village stakes.

entries.title: Ovenhold
entries.summary: Ovenhold is an ancient mortal kingdom of hearths, ovens, labor, survival, craft, and cooked food.
entries.internalLore: Ovenhold stood opposite the Faery Realm during the 300 Year War. It represents mortal craft, hearth work, cooked food, endurance, and the stubborn labor of survival. Its conflict with the Faery Realm ended only when the Tablemaker prepared the meal that neither side could refuse.

fields.Core Imagery: Hearths, ovens, stone kitchens, craft, smoke, bread, labor.
fields.Conflict: Fought the Faery Realm for 300 years.

timelineEvents: The Meal That Ended the War

entries.title: The Everfeast
entries.summary: The Everfeast is the heavenly culinary realm where the Tablemaker returned after his sacrifice.
entries.internalLore: The Everfeast preserves meals in their perfect form. After the Tablemaker's final meal ended the 300 Year War and cost him his mortal life, his spirit returned to The Everfeast. Before returning, he released Food Essence into the world, making meals and magical cooking sacred.

fields.Story Use: Ancient faith, myth, and magical cooking origin.

timelineEvents: The Meal That Ended the War

entries.title: Food Essence
entries.summary: Food Essence is the sacred culinary force released into the world by the Tablemaker after the meal that ended the 300 Year War.
entries.internalLore: Food Essence is not a normal ingredient. It is the spiritual and magical foundation that makes meals sacred and allows culinary magic to matter. It entered the world after the Tablemaker's final meal and is tied to Passion, Taste, and Love. Dark Culinary Arts and Mas'eel FEAST doctrine are corruptions or distortions of what Food Essence is meant to be.

fields.Not A Pantry Ingredient: Track as a magic system, not a cookable item.
fields.Corrupted By: Dark Culinary Arts, Mas'eel FEAST doctrine

entries.title: Whisken Saints
entries.summary: A Whisken cultural thread about holy figures and old teachers who helped shape the people into a hearth-centered culture.
entries.internalLore: The Whisken Saints material should be organized as cultural lore rather than treated as a separate religion. It frames older Whisken history as a movement from tooth, instinct, hunger, and isolation toward hearth, table, mercy, and shared food. It should harmonize with the wider Tablemaker faith and the Three Pillars of Love, Passion, and Taste.

fields.Needs Rewrite: Keep the useful cultural shape, but reconcile it with current Cat Cauldron and Tablemaker canon.
fields.Culture Use: Stories told around tavern fires, harvests, and feast days.

entries.title: Mas'eel False Traders
entries.summary: Mas'eel cultists came to Tabby Island pretending to be traders, using food and trust to gain power over the Whisken.
entries.internalLore: After Tohm awakened the Cat Cauldron, the Mas'eel sensed its power and sailed to Tabby Island disguised as traders. They introduced new foods, built trust over years, secretly seared the island, gained influence in Whisken village ranks, and persecuted Whisken people for following the Triadic faith taught by the Tablemaker.

fields.True Goal: Find the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's magical recipes.

secrets: Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island

entries.title: Lel Kai's Rescue Fleet
entries.summary: Tohm asks Lel Kai to send boats to rescue the Whisken from Tabby Island during the second exodus.
entries.internalLore: When Tohm hears what is happening on Tabby Island, he gets his friend Lel Kai, who is becoming general of the faery army, to gather boats and save as many Whisken as possible. The island's corruption is already severe, scattering many boats. The known survivors become the Whisken who now live in Whisker Woods and Whisken Village.

fields.Known Survivors: Current Whisken in Whisker Woods / Whisken Village.
fields.Story Function: Explains why the current village is incomplete, displaced, and haunted by missing kin.

entries.title: Lillia's Camp
entries.summary: Lillia's camp in the Faery Realm lets her use ambient magic to mass-produce Dark Culinary Arts.
entries.internalLore: Lillia camps in the Faery Realm because the ambient magic allows her to produce corrupted meals at scale instead of infusing each dish one at a time. The Mas'eel connection gives this camp a broader villain network: Lillia wants power and magical transformation, while the Mas'eel hunt for the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's recipes.

fields.Gameplay Role: Late-story enemy production site and corrupted food source.
fields.Villain Link: Princess Lillia and the Mas'eel Cult.

entries.title: Leirbag
entries.summary: Leirbag is a Notion-sourced Mas'eel influence that should be reviewed before becoming hard canon.
entries.internalLore: Notion lore says the Mas'eel Cult's skewed view of the Tablemaker was inspired by a Dark Angel called Leirbag. Keep this as a review slot rather than fully resolved canon until its role, origin, and relationship to the Tablemaker faith are clarified.

fields.Possible Role: Origin or corrupter of Mas'eel FEAST doctrine.
fields.Do Not Confuse With: Discarded older mythic names are not canon.

entries.title: Festival of Full Plates
entries.summary: A yearly Whisken feast celebrating balance, gratitude, remembrance, and the promise that no plate should go empty.
entries.internalLore: The Festival of Full Plates is a Whisken celebration of balance, gratitude, remembrance, and shared food. It ties the village's tavern culture to the Tablemaker's Triadic faith. This should stay distinct from the Mas'eel corruption of FEAST: the festival fills plates to share, while FEAST hoards and consumes.

fields.ingredientsRequired: Whisken Hearth Stew, Healthy Ale, Potato, Turnip, Specialty Herbs
fields.Faith Meaning: Shared abundance under Passion, Taste, and Love.

entries.title: Healthy Ale
entries.summary: A famous Whisken ale invented by Oswin; despite the name, it is still not especially healthy.
entries.internalLore: Healthy Ale is a village-famous drink tied to Oswin's odd remedies and Whisken brewing culture. It can be funny, useful, and slightly suspect: villagers rely on Oswin, but the drink's name should not be taken too literally.

fields.ingredientsRequired: Whisken Root Ferment, Moonlit Dew, Specialty Herbs
fields.gameplayEffect: Possible stamina, warmth, or light recovery buff with comedic side effects.

entries.title: Whisken Hearth Stew
entries.summary: A hearty Whisken village stew built from roots, herbs, broth, and whatever the village can safely gather.
entries.internalLore: Whisken Hearth Stew is a practical village meal and a good starter recipe slot for the Pantry. It can represent the Whisken instinct to feed everyone first, especially after displacement and scattered rescue boats made food security a sacred concern.

fields.ingredientsRequired: Potato, Turnip, Boga, Specialty Herbs, Cat Cauldron Broth Base
fields.gameplayEffect: Comfort meal, basic recovery, village reputation boost.

entries.title: Cat Cauldron Broth Base
entries.summary: A flexible broth base made in the Cat Cauldron and used to finalize stews, magical meals, and comfort dishes.
entries.internalLore: This is a practical Pantry slot for the Cat Cauldron's everyday cooking function. It should separate normal broth use from the catastrophic lore of awakening the ancient cauldron. Gwen can use it as a meal component while the deeper truth remains hidden.

fields.ingredientsRequired: Any Meat / Creature Drop, Any Produce, Specialty Herbs
fields.gameplayEffect: Component for stews, magical meals, and restorative recipes.

entries.title: False Trader Spice
entries.summary: A suspicious spice blend introduced by Mas'eel false traders during their slow infiltration of Tabby Island.
entries.internalLore: False Trader Spice is a practical ingredient slot for the Mas'eel infiltration. It appears harmless or exciting as a new imported flavor, but is tied to the cult's slow searing of Tabby Island and their corruption of Whisken faith and food culture.

fields.gameplayUse: Quest clue, corrupted recipe component, possible debuff ingredient.

secrets: Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island

entries.title: Moonlit Dew
entries.summary: A night-gathered dew used in Whisken ales, tonics, and gentle infusions.
entries.internalLore: Moonlit Dew fits Whisken brewing traditions and gives Oswin, Mona, and the village a gentle night-gathering ingredient for ales and tonics.

fields.whereFound: Whisker Woods, Whisken Village orchards at night

entries.title: Whisken Root Ferment
entries.summary: A fermented root base used in Whisken ales, tonics, and tavern drinks.
entries.internalLore: Whisken Root Ferment is a practical Pantry ingredient for village brewing. It supports Healthy Ale, festival drinks, and Oswin's odd remedies without needing to invent a new system.

fields.whereFound: Whisken Village cellars, Whisker Woods roots
fields.usedInRecipes: Healthy Ale, Festival of Full Plates

entries.title: Boar Meat
entries.summary: A hearty meat drop from Thornback Boars, useful for stews and combat meals.

fields.usedInRecipes: Whisken Hearth Stew, Cat Cauldron Broth Base

entries.title: Mushroom Bits
entries.summary: Earthy mushroom pieces gathered from Mushgrubs or mushroom-heavy paths.

fields.usedInRecipes: Cat Cauldron Broth Base, Whisken Hearth Stew

entries.title: Mushgrub Jelly
entries.summary: A soft jelly drop from Mushgrubs that can bind sauces, broths, and bait recipes.

entries.title: Honey Globs
entries.summary: Sticky honey globs from Honeybloats, useful for sweets, ales, and trap-like recipes.

fields.usedInRecipes: Healthy Ale, sweet meals, sticky trap recipes

entries.title: Dusk Slime Gel
entries.summary: Dusk Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Dusk Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the dusk flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Bitter Slime Gel
entries.summary: Bitter Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Bitter Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the bitter flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Sweet Slime Gel
entries.summary: Sweet Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Sweet Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the sweet flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Savory Slime Gel
entries.summary: Savory Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Savory Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the savory flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Sour Slime Gel
entries.summary: Sour Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Sour Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the sour flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Salty Slime Gel
entries.summary: Salty Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Salty Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the salty flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Spicy Slime Gel
entries.summary: Spicy Slime Gel is a flavor-aspected slime drop used in magical meals, substitutions, ales, and tonics.
entries.internalLore: Spicy Slime Gel gives the Pantry a concrete ingredient slot for the spicy flavor branch of the Slime Flavor / Element System.

fields.usedInRecipes: Slime Substitutions, Magical Meals, Ales / Tonics

entries.title: Ancient Whisken Create the Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: Ancient Whisken seekers create the Cat Cauldron while trying to improve food and reach the knowledge of what is untasted.

entries.title: First Whisken Exodus
entries.summary: The Cat Cauldron causes Tabby Island to begin decaying, forcing the ancient Whisken to flee for the first time.

entries.title: Cat Cauldron Is Buried and Forgotten
entries.summary: The ancient Whisken lock the Cat Cauldron at the bottom of Tabby Island, remove it from history books, and stop speaking of it until it is forgotten.

entries.title: Tablemaker Stories Inspire Tohm
entries.summary: Stories of the Tablemaker, also called the Master Chef, are told to young Tohm and seed his obsession with sacred meals and untasted food.

entries.title: Tohm Grows Up on Tabby Island
entries.summary: Tohm lives among the Whisken people on Tabby Island.

entries.title: Tohm Becomes Obsessed with Magical Food
entries.summary: Because he has sweet taste buds and has tasted every normal food, Tohm becomes obsessed with tasting magical food.

entries.title: Tohm Awakens the Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: Tohm discovers the forgotten Cat Cauldron, cooks a meal in it, releases a pulse into the earth, and begins Tabby Island's second decay.

entries.title: Mas'eel Sense the Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: The pulse from the awakened Cat Cauldron lets the Mas'eel Cult sense its power and know it was on Tabby Island.

entries.title: Tohm Flees With the Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: Tohm takes the Cat Cauldron and flees in the Living Tavern without anyone knowing.

entries.title: Mas'eel Infiltrate Tabby Island
entries.summary: Mas'eel cultists sail to Tabby Island pretending to be traders, introduce new foods, gain power, and persecute the Whisken for their Triadic faith.

entries.title: Second Whisken Exodus
entries.summary: The Whisken flee Tabby Island again, though to them it feels like the first time, while Lel Kai's rescue boats are scattered by corruption.

entries.title: Survivors Reach Whisker Woods
entries.summary: The known Whisken survivors reach Whisker Woods and establish the current Whisken Village.

entries.title: Tohm Rebuilds in Whisker Woods
entries.summary: Tohm establishes himself in Whisker Woods and later becomes a renowned chef and food critic while carrying guilt over the Cat Cauldron.

entries.title: Lillia Wants to Become a Faery
entries.summary: Princess Lillia becomes obsessed with gaining magic and becoming like a faery.

entries.title: Faeries Refuse to Help
entries.summary: The faeries refuse to give humans magic because they fear humans would dominate the world.

entries.title: King Takes the Dragon Knife
entries.summary: The king wages war against the dwarves and takes the magical dragon knife.

entries.title: Tohm Gains Access to Dragon Knife
entries.summary: Tohm gains access to the royal family's spoils / dragon knife through a food contest or royal arrangement.

entries.title: Lillia Consumes the Unstable Magical Dish
entries.summary: Tohm creates an unstable magical dish. Lillia consumes it and gains dangerous powers.

entries.title: Lillia Tears Out Recipe Pages
entries.summary: Lillia attempts to take Tohm's recipe book, but Tohm snatches it back after the living chicken tavern stands up. Lillia tears out several pages.

entries.title: Tohm Flees
entries.summary: Guards approach and Tohm is forced to flee.

entries.title: Lillia Begins Using Dark Culinary Arts
entries.summary: Lillia uses torn recipe pages and magic to create corrupted meals and empower others.

entries.title: Gwen Is Recruited
entries.summary: Gwen is recruited by Tohm and becomes involved in recovering the recipe pages.

entries.title: Kap's Pond Is Corrupted
entries.summary: Gwen encounters corruption spreading through Whisker Woods when Kap is attacked near the pond.

entries.title: Secret: Tohm Awakened the Cat Cauldron
entries.summary: Tohm activated the buried Cat Cauldron, restarted Tabby Island's decay, drew the Mas'eel Cult's attention, and fled with the artifact.
entries.internalLore: Tohm activated the buried Cat Cauldron, restarted Tabby Island's decay, drew the Mas'eel Cult's attention, and fled with the artifact.

entries.title: Secret: Public Does Not Know Tohm Caused Disaster
entries.summary: The public does not know Tohm awakened the Cat Cauldron, removed it from Tabby Island, and triggered the second decay.
entries.internalLore: The public does not know Tohm awakened the Cat Cauldron, removed it from Tabby Island, and triggered the second decay.

entries.title: Secret: Tohm Never Drinks From The Cauldron
entries.summary: Tohm never drinks from the cauldron.
entries.internalLore: Tohm never drinks from the cauldron.

entries.title: Secret: Cat Cauldron Beneath Tabby Island
entries.summary: The ancient Whisken created the Cat Cauldron, buried it beneath Tabby Island, erased it from their history, and forgot it.
entries.internalLore: The ancient Whisken created the Cat Cauldron, buried it beneath Tabby Island, erased it from their history, and forgot it.

entries.title: Secret: Mas'eel Infiltrated Tabby Island
entries.summary: The Mas'eel arrived as false traders, introduced new foods, gained village power, persecuted Triadic Whisken, and then left to hunt the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's recipes with Princess Lillia.
entries.internalLore: The Mas'eel arrived as false traders, introduced new foods, gained village power, persecuted Triadic Whisken, and then left to hunt the Cat Cauldron and Tohm's recipes with Princess Lillia.

entries.title: Secret: Lillia Tore Recipe Pages
entries.summary: Lillia tore pages from Tohm's recipe book.
entries.internalLore: Lillia tore pages from Tohm's recipe book.

entries.title: Secret: Lillia Uses Dark Culinary Arts
entries.summary: Lillia uses the Dark Culinary Arts to corrupt food.
entries.internalLore: Lillia uses the Dark Culinary Arts to corrupt food.

entries.title: Secret: King's Obsession Caused War
entries.summary: The king's obsession with giving Lillia magic caused war with the dwarves.
entries.internalLore: The king's obsession with giving Lillia magic caused war with the dwarves.

entries.title: Secret: Faeries Refused Human Magic
entries.summary: The faeries refused to help give humans magic because they feared humans would become too powerful.
entries.internalLore: The faeries refused to help give humans magic because they feared humans would become too powerful.

entries.title: Secret: Dark Culinary Arts Can Corrupt Consumers
entries.summary: Dark Culinary Arts can make consumers evil or corrupted.
entries.internalLore: Dark Culinary Arts can make consumers evil or corrupted.

entries.title: Secret: Slimes Born From Nutrients Or Corruption
entries.summary: Slimes are born from excess nutrients or excess corruption depending on their type.
entries.internalLore: Slimes are born from excess nutrients or excess corruption depending on their type.

entries.title: Public Gwen Description
entries.summary: Gwen is a hardworking young fighter from Osul who finds herself swept into a strange culinary adventure after joining Tohm Kyatt's tavern.
entries.publicDescription: Gwen is a hardworking young fighter from Osul who finds herself swept into a strange culinary adventure after joining Tohm Kyatt's tavern. Armed with her sword, basket, and appetite, she gathers ingredients, protects villages, and cooks magical meals to face the dangers spreading through Whisker Woods.

fields.Suggested Post Ideas: Gwen with sword and basket; Gwen cooking after a fight; potato-themed character post.

entries.title: Public Tohm Description
entries.summary: Tohm Kyatt is a world-renowned Whisken chef and food critic whose mysterious recipes may be the key to saving the land.
entries.publicDescription: Tohm Kyatt is a world-renowned Whisken chef and food critic whose mysterious recipes may be the key to saving the land. Brilliant, dramatic, and more than a little secretive, Tohm recruits Gwen to help recover stolen recipes before they cause even more chaos.

fields.Suggested Post Ideas: Mysterious chef profile; critic quote graphic; tavern recruitment tease.

entries.title: Public Lillia Description
entries.summary: Princess Lillia is a magical threat tied to the rise of corrupted cooking and dangerous recipe-powered enemies.
entries.publicDescription: Princess Lillia is a magical threat tied to the rise of corrupted cooking and dangerous recipe-powered enemies. Her obsession with magic has left a trail of strange meals, twisted powers, and chaos across the land.

fields.Suggested Post Ideas: Corrupted recipe tease; mysterious princess silhouette; magical cooking danger post.

entries.title: Public Slime Description
entries.summary: Slimes are colorful flavor-filled creatures born from the excess nutrients of healthy forests.
entries.publicDescription: Slimes are colorful flavor-filled creatures born from the excess nutrients of healthy forests. Depending on where they form, they may taste like berries, herbs, fruit, or something far stranger. Some are friendly, some flee, and some are best avoided.

fields.Suggested Post Ideas: Flavor chart; red and blue slime comparison; friendly vs hostile slime behaviors.

entries.title: Old Version: Tohm Builds Trust with King Over Months
entries.summary: In an older version, Tohm slowly gained the king's trust over months in order to access the dragon knife.
entries.internalLore: This version is archived for reference and should not override the newer royal food contest idea unless revived.

entries.title: Newer Version: Tohm Wins Royal Food Contest
entries.summary: In a newer version, the king creates a nationwide cooking contest, and Tohm wins, giving him access to royal spoils including the dragon knife.
entries.internalLore: This is the newer preferred access path, but it still needs exact quest/story structure.
